title: Nonperturbative renormalization of Haldane pseudopotentials from the exact two-electron spectrum
authors: G. -Q. Hai, M. T. Matsubara, L. Cândido, B. G. A. Brito
abstract: Haldane pseudopotentials $V_{|m|}$ provide the effective interaction parameters governing correlated states in the fractional quantum Hall regime. In conventional formulations, these quantities are obtained by projecting the Coulomb interaction onto relative-angular-momentum states within the lowest Landau level, thereby neglecting virtual transitions to higher Landau levels. Here, we formulate a nonperturbative description of effective interactions directly from the exact two-electron spectrum in a magnetic field. By solving the relative-motion problem beyond the lowest-Landau-level approximation, we define renormalized pseudopotentials $V^*_{|m|}$ from the exact eigenenergies and introduce dynamical corrections $Δ_{|m|}=V^*_{|m|}-V_{|m|}$. The corrections remain systematically negative and depend strongly on both interaction strength and relative angular momentum, reflecting dynamical correlation effects associated with higher-state virtual admixture. The exact results reproduce the perturbative Landau-level-mixing limit at weak coupling while exhibiting substantial deviations in the strong-mixing regime, signaling the breakdown of low-order perturbative expansions. In particular, the short-range interaction channels relevant to Laughlin-type correlations undergo strong renormalization, leading to substantial modification of the effective interaction hierarchy in strongly interacting systems such as ZnO/MgZnO heterostructures. The present formulation establishes a microscopic framework for incorporating nonperturbative Landau-level-mixing effects into effective interaction theories of quantum Hall systems.
summary: This work establishes a nonperturbative framework to calculate effective electron interactions in the fractional quantum Hall regime by going beyond the standard Lowest Landau Level approximation. By using the exact two-electron spectrum, the authors derive renormalized pseudopotentials that incorporate dynamical correlations from higher Landau levels. This shows that strong interactions significantly modify the effective interaction hierarchy, a crucial finding for understanding strongly correlated materials like ZnO/MgZnO heterostructures.
main_problem: To develop a nonperturbative description of effective electron-electron interactions (Haldane pseudopotentials) in the fractional quantum Hall regime by accounting for virtual transitions to higher Landau levels.
main_result: The exact renormalized pseudopotentials ($V^*|m|$) show a systematic, negative dynamical correction ($\Delta|m|$) compared to the standard Lowest Landau Level (LLL) approximation, which is crucial in strongly interacting systems.
method: The authors solve the relative-motion problem using the exact two-electron spectrum in a magnetic field, defining renormalized pseudopotentials directly from the exact eigenenergies.
model_or_system: The system is two interacting electrons confined to a 2D plane under a perpendicular magnetic field, analyzed via the relative-motion Hamiltonian which separates into center-of-mass and relative-motion sectors.
key_observables: Renormalized pseudopotentials ($V^*|m|$), dynamical correction ($\Delta|m|$), and the difference between short-range interaction scales ($\delta^*_{13}$).
important_parameters: Dimensionless interaction strength ($\gamma_B$), and characteristic thresholds ($\gamma_c^B(m)$) marking the onset of strong Landau-level mixing.
main_assumptions: The conventional pseudopotentials are only valid in the LLL approximation; the exact formulation is necessary when Landau-level mixing is nonperturbative.
figures_summary: Figures compare the LLL pseudopotentials ($V|m|$) against the exact renormalized values ($V^*|m|$) across various angular momenta ($m$) and interaction strengths ($\gamma_B$), highlighting the magnitude of the correction $\Delta|m|$.
paper_structure: The paper contrasts the conventional LLL projection method with the exact spectrum approach, deriving the renormalized pseudopotentials and quantifying the dynamical correction $\Delta|m|$ which reveals the breakdown of perturbation theory in the strong-mixing regime.
why_interesting: The methodology of deriving effective interactions from the exact spectrum, rather than relying on perturbative expansions, provides a powerful, nonperturbative tool applicable to strongly correlated electron systems where simple approximations fail.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Renormalized pseudopotentials V ∗ |m| (solid lines) and conventional Haldane pseudopotentials V|m| (dash lines) as functions of interaction strength γB for m = 0, −1, −2, ..., −9. The exact pseudopotentials remain systematically smaller than the LLL-projected values due to dynamical correlation effects associated with higher-Landau-level virtual processes. The shaded region corresponds to V ∗ |m| > 1 or equivalently Erel 0m > 3/2 where the lowest relative branch enters the higher- sublevel energy window. Red semi-dots indicate the threshold values γc B(m) = p

FIG. 3. Renormalization of the short-range pseudopotential difference relevant to the Laughlin ν = 1/3 state. The figure compares the conventional LLL difference δLLL 13 = V1 −V3 (black-solid line), the renormalized difference δ∗ 13 = V ∗ 1 −V ∗ 3 (red-solid line), and the dynamical correction δ∆= |∆1 −∆3| (blue-dashed line) as functions of γB.

FIG. 4. Exact dynamical correction ∆|m| (solid lines) and perturbative Landau-level-mixing correction κ2V2(m) from Ref. [8] (dash lines) as functions of the interaction strength of γB for m=0,-1,-2,..., -9. At small γB, the exact results agree quantitatively with perturbation theory. At larger γB, substantial deviations emerge. Red semi-dots indicate the threshold values γc B(m).
